content.problemStatement:
Tzuyu gave Nayeon a strip of NNN cells (numbered 111 through NNN) for her birthday. This strip is described by a sequence A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã, where for each valid iii, the iii-th cell is blocked if Ai=1A_i = 1Ai‚Äã=1 or free if Ai=0A_i = 0Ai‚Äã=0. Tzuyu and Nayeon are going to use it to play a game with the following rules:


The players alternate turns; Nayeon plays first.
Initially, both players are outside of the strip. However, note that afterwards during the game, their positions are always different.
In each turn, the current player should choose a free cell and move there. Afterwards, this cell becomes blocked and the players cannot move to it again.
If it is the current player's first turn, she may move to any free cell.
Otherwise, she may only move to one of the left and right adjacent cells, i.e. from a cell ccc, the current player may only move to the cell c‚àí1c-1c‚àí1 or c+1c+1c+1 (if it is free).
If a player is unable to move to a free cell during her turn, this player loses the game.
Nayeon and Tzuyu are very smart, so they both play optimally. Since it is Nayeon's birthday, she wants to know if she can beat Tzuyu. Find out who wins.
constraints: the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 10610^6106
Subtasks**Subtask #1 (50 points):** Ai=0A_i = 0Ai‚Äã=0 for each iii (2‚â§i‚â§N‚àí12 \le i \le N-12‚â§i‚â§N‚àí1)
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing the string `"Yes"` if Nayeon wins the game or `"No"` if Tzuyu wins (without quotes).

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: Since both Nayeon and Tzuyu play optimally, Nayeon can start e.g. by moving to cell 444, which then becomes blocked. Tzuyu has to pick either the cell 333 or the cell 555, which also becomes blocked. Nayeon is then left with only one empty cell next to cell 444 (the one Tzuyu did not pick); after she moves there, Tzuyu is unable to move, so she loses the game.
Example case 2: Regardless of what cell Nayeon moves to at the start, Tzuyu will always be able to beat her.
